acceptance: A Next.js App Router TypeScript project is bootstrapped in the repository.
The root page loads as the todo app primary screen.
The project exposes npm run lint, npm test, and npm run build scripts that all pass on the baseline app.
Test tooling is configured with at least one smoke test so npm test succeeds before feature tests exist.
The app layout is usable on desktop and mobile widths.
issues.body: Bootstrap the repository as a Next.js App Router TypeScript project. Add the primary todo app shell, baseline responsive styling, page metadata, lint/build/test scripts, Vitest or equivalent test tooling, and at least one smoke test so Looper verification passes before feature work begins.
issues.title: Create the todo app shell

acceptance: Each todo contains id, title, completed, createdAt, and updatedAt fields.
Todos are loaded from localStorage on the client.
Todos are saved to localStorage after mutations.
Storage failures do not crash the UI-facing API.
issues.body: Create a small typed todo domain module and browser-local persistence adapter backed by localStorage. Include unique IDs, title, completion status, created timestamp, updated timestamp, load/save APIs, and graceful handling for unavailable or failing storage.
test_plan: Add unit tests for loading empty storage, loading saved todos, saving todos, and storage failure handling.
issues.title: Implement typed todo storage

acceptance: Creating a todo adds it immediately to state.
Editing a todo updates its title and updated timestamp.
Completing and restoring toggle completion status correctly.
Deleting removes the todo permanently from persisted state.
Todos persist after page refresh.
issues.body: Build the todo state/controller layer that hydrates from storage and provides create, edit, complete, restore, and delete actions with timestamp updates and persistence after every successful mutation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for create, edit, complete, restore, delete, timestamp changes, and reload persistence.
issues.title: Implement todo state actions

acceptance: New todo titles are trimmed before saving.
Empty and whitespace-only new todos are rejected.
Edited todo titles are trimmed before saving.
Empty and whitespace-only edited titles are rejected.
Validation messages are visible near the relevant input.
issues.body: Apply shared title normalization and validation for creating and editing todos. Trim titles before saving and reject empty or whitespace-only values with clear validation messages.
test_plan: Add tests for valid trimmed titles, empty create rejection, whitespace create rejection, empty edit rejection, and whitespace edit rejection.
issues.title: Enforce todo title validation

acceptance: A user can enter a valid title and add a todo.
The added todo appears in the list immediately.
Completed todos are visually distinct from active todos.
The UI remains readable and usable with multiple todos.
issues.body: Implement the visible todo creation form and list rendering using the todo state layer. Active and completed todos should be visually distinct, and newly added valid todos should appear immediately.
test_plan: Add component tests for adding a todo and rendering active versus completed items.
issues.title: Build add form and todo list UI

acceptance: A user can enter edit mode for a todo.
Saving a valid edit updates the displayed title immediately.
The edited title persists after refresh.
Invalid edited titles show a clear validation message and are not saved.
A user can cancel editing without changing the todo.
issues.body: Implement an inline or focused edit state for existing todos, including save and cancel behavior, keyboard-friendly controls, validation feedback, and persistence of edited titles.
test_plan: Add component tests for editing, edit persistence, invalid edit validation, and cancel behavior.
issues.title: Add inline todo editing

acceptance: A user can mark an active todo complete.
A user can restore a completed todo to active.
A user can delete a todo.
Deleted todos no longer appear after refresh.
issues.body: Implement controls for marking active todos complete, restoring completed todos to active, and permanently deleting todos.
test_plan: Add component tests for complete, restore, delete, and deleted-after-reload behavior.
issues.title: Add complete restore and delete controls

acceptance: A user can filter todos by all, active, and completed.
A user can search todos by title case-insensitively.
Search and filter combinations return only todos matching both conditions.
Changing search or filter updates the list immediately.
issues.body: Add case-insensitive title search and all, active, and completed filters. Ensure search and filter state combine as an intersection of matching todos.
test_plan: Add component tests for all filter states, case-insensitive search, and search-plus-filter intersections.
issues.title: Implement search and status filters

acceptance: The app shows a no-todos empty state when the todo list is empty.
The app shows a no-active-todos empty state when the active filter has no results.
The app shows a no-completed-todos empty state when the completed filter has no results.
The app shows a no-search-results empty state when search returns no matches.
Empty states do not appear when matching todos exist.
issues.body: Implement helpful empty states for no todos, no active todos, no completed todos, and no search results. Empty states should reflect the current filter and search context.
test_plan: Add component tests for each empty state condition.
issues.title: Add contextual empty states

acceptance: Core create, edit, complete, restore, delete, search, filter, validation, and empty-state behavior is covered by automated tests.
Persistence across reloads is covered by automated tests.
Storage failure handling is covered by automated tests.
All tests pass locally.
issues.body: Add or complete automated unit and component tests covering create, edit, complete, restore, delete, search, filter, validation, persistence, storage errors, and empty-state behavior.
issues.title: Cover core workflows with automated tests

acceptance: npm run build succeeds without local-only assumptions.
The deployed preview exposes the complete working todo app.
Preview data is browser-local and not shared between users.
No authentication, database, or secret configuration is required.
issues.body: Ensure the app can be deployed to a public preview environment with production build settings and no required private credentials. Document the preview deployment path or configure the target platform used by Looper.
test_plan: Open the preview URL and verify add, refresh persistence, filter, search, edit, complete, restore, and delete manually.
issues.title: Prepare public preview deployment
